actionable_predicate: Run the four-medium audit on every interface labelled a 'contract' in your roadmap docs. For each contract, write one sentence answering each of: (1) does it survive a rewrite (uncut by śastra)? (2) does it survive an architectural fashion cycle (unburned by pāvaka)? (3) does it survive accumulated tech debt (undrowned by āpaḥ)? (4) does it survive a quarter of neglect (undried by māruta)? Contracts that fail any of the four are flagged as substrate-coupled rather than durable.
example_decision: Q4 platform migration: Kubernetes→Nomad. PM directive: before the migration starts, the platform team produces a four-medium audit of every 'contract' in the company. The customer-facing HTTP contracts (/v1/charge, /v1/refund) pass all four. The Helm-chart-style internal contracts fail śastra (they break under refactor). The orchestrator-coupled service-discovery contracts fail pāvaka (they were written for a specific architectural moment). PM's roadmap distinguishes 'durable contract migration' (low-risk) from 'substrate replacement' (high-risk requiring re-implementation, not migration).
objection: The four-medium audit is itself work. PM-side caveat: do this audit only for the company's load-bearing trunk contracts, not for every internal interface. The verse explicitly does not require every interface to meet the four-medium spec. PASS as a real PM discipline.
